era_true.statement: Rick Deckard is a bounty hunter who retires androids.
Rick Deckard works for the San Francisco Police Department.
Rick Deckard uses a Voigt-Kampff machine to test for androids.
The Voigt-Kampff test measures empathic responses.
The Voigt-Kampff test monitors capillary dilation and blush response.
Rick Deckard's superior at the SFPD is Inspector Harry Bryant.
Dave Holden is the bounty hunter shot by an android before Deckard takes the assignment.
Leon Kowalski shoots Dave Holden during a Voigt-Kampff test.
Rachael Rosen is a Nexus-6 android produced by the Tyrell Corporation.
Rachael does not initially know she is an android.
Rachael's memories are implanted from a real human's experiences.
Eldon Tyrell is the founder and head of the Tyrell Corporation.
Eldon Tyrell lives in a massive pyramid-shaped building.
Roy Batty is a Nexus-6 combat model replicant.
Roy Batty is the leader of the rogue replicant group.
Roy Batty kills Eldon Tyrell by crushing his skull.
Roy Batty pushes his thumbs into Tyrell's eyes.
Pris Stratton is a basic pleasure model replicant.
Pris hides among the toys in J.F. Sebastian's apartment.
Zhora Salome works as an exotic dancer using a snake in her act.
Deckard shoots Zhora as she runs through plate glass windows.
Leon Kowalski nearly kills Deckard before Rachael shoots Leon.
Rachael saves Deckard's life by shooting Leon Kowalski.
J.F. Sebastian is a genetic designer who works for the Tyrell Corporation.
J.F. Sebastian suffers from Methuselah Syndrome, a form of accelerated aging.
J.F. Sebastian lives alone in the Bradbury Building.
J.F. Sebastian creates living toys to keep himself company.
Roy Batty forces J.F. Sebastian to bring him to Tyrell.
Nexus-6 replicants have a built-in four-year lifespan.
The four-year lifespan is a failsafe to prevent emotional development.
Replicants are banned from Earth under penalty of death.
The enforcement of replicant termination is called retirement.
Gaff is a police officer who accompanies Deckard throughout the case.
Gaff makes small origami figures throughout the film.
Gaff leaves a unicorn origami outside Deckard's apartment.
Gaff tells Deckard that Rachael will not live because she has no termination date.
Gaff says the line "Too bad she won't live, but then again who does."
Roy Batty delivers a monologue about attack ships on fire off the shoulder of Orion.
Roy Batty mentions C-beams glittering in the dark near the Tannhäuser Gate.
Roy Batty dies on a rooftop while holding a white dove.
Roy Batty saves Deckard's life moments before dying.
Roy Batty pulls Deckard up from the edge of a building.
Roy Batty drives a nail through his own hand to maintain feeling.
Deckard and Rachael flee together at the end of the film.
The Tyrell Corporation motto is "More human than human."
The Tyrell Corporation manufactures Nexus-6 replicants.
Replicants are bioengineered beings made of organic material.
Replicants possess superhuman strength and endurance.
Replicants are virtually identical to humans in appearance.
The Voigt-Kampff test is the primary method for identifying replicants.
Los Angeles in 2019 is depicted as a dark, rain-soaked, overcrowded city.
Massive video billboards dominate the Los Angeles skyline.
A giant advertisement for Coca-Cola appears on a building in Los Angeles.
Off-world colonies exist where replicants serve as slave labor.
Spinner vehicles are flying police cars used in Los Angeles.
The streets of Los Angeles are filled with a multicultural, multilingual population.
Acid rain falls regularly on the city of Los Angeles.
Deckard is called out of retirement to hunt the escaped replicants.
Six replicants originally escaped, but two were killed before the film begins.
Deckard must retire four remaining replicants during the film.
Leon keeps precious photographs in his hotel room.
Replicants value photographs because they substitute for real memories.
Rachael plays piano in Deckard's apartment.
Rachael lets down her hair in Deckard's apartment.
Tyrell wears thick glasses and resembles an owl-like figure.
An artificial owl sits in the Tyrell Corporation headquarters.
Rachael asks Deckard if he has ever taken the Voigt-Kampff test himself.
Deckard visits the Tyrell Corporation to test the Voigt-Kampff on a Nexus-6.
Deckard initially tests Rachael without knowing she is a replicant.
It takes over one hundred questions for the Voigt-Kampff to identify Rachael.
Tyrell calls Rachael an experiment with implanted memories.
Pris applies black paint across her eyes as a disguise.
Pris attacks Deckard by wrapping her legs around his neck.
Deckard shoots Pris multiple times to retire her.
Roy Batty howls in anguish when he discovers Pris is dead.
Roy Batty hunts Deckard through the Bradbury Building.
Roy Batty strips to the waist during his pursuit of Deckard.
Deckard uses a standard-issue blaster to retire replicants.
Leon asks Deckard how it feels to live in fear during their confrontation.
Zhora is identified through analysis of one of her snake's scales.
Deckard tracks Zhora to a strip club called Taffey Lewis's bar.
Taffey Lewis is the owner of the bar where Zhora performs.
Deckard pretends to be from a committee on moral abuses to question Taffey Lewis.
The Esper machine in Deckard's apartment can enhance and navigate within photographs.
Deckard uses the Esper machine to find a clue in one of Leon's photographs.
The cityscape features enormous industrial towers emitting flames.
Asian cultural influences pervade the street-level culture of Los Angeles.
Noodle bars operate on the rain-soaked streets of Los Angeles.
Deckard eats at a noodle bar at the beginning of the film.
Blimp-like vehicles float above the city advertising off-world colony life.
The off-world colonies are promoted as a chance for a new life.
Replicants were designed for hazardous work in off-world colonies.
In the novel, Rick Deckard's wife is named Iran Deckard.
Iran Deckard uses a Penfield mood organ to regulate her emotions.
The Penfield mood organ allows users to dial specific emotional states.
In the novel, Mercerism is the dominant religion on Earth.
Mercerism centers on the suffering of a figure named Wilbur Mercer.
Followers of Mercerism use empathy boxes to share collective experiences.
An empathy box allows users to fuse with Wilbur Mercer's consciousness.
In the novel, Buster Friendly is a popular television and radio host.
Buster Friendly reveals on air that Mercerism is a fraud.
In the novel, most animal species are extinct or extremely rare.
Owning a real animal is a major status symbol in the novel.
Rick Deckard owns an electric sheep at the beginning of the novel.
Deckard desperately wants to own a real, living animal.
Deckard purchases a real goat with his bounty earnings in the novel.
The goat is later pushed off the roof by Rachael Rosen in the novel.
In the novel, the Rosen Association manufactures the Nexus-6 androids.
Kipple is a term in the novel for useless junk that accumulates in abandoned spaces.
J.R. Isidore is a character in the novel who lives alone in an abandoned building.
J.R. Isidore is classified as a special, someone with diminished cognitive abilities.
J.R. Isidore befriends Pris Stratton in the novel.
Nuclear fallout called dust covers much of the Earth in the novel.
Many humans have emigrated from Earth to off-world colonies in the novel.
People who remain on Earth are often those who failed genetic testing.
Specials are humans whose genes have been damaged by radioactive dust.
The term chickenhead is a derogatory name for specials in the novel.
Androids in the novel lack genuine empathy for other living beings.
The inability to feel empathy is what distinguishes androids from humans in the novel.
Deckard begins to question whether retiring androids is morally justified.

era_false.statement: Rick Deckard is a retired military officer living in New York City when he is called back to duty.
Rachael Rosen is a human woman who pretends to be a replicant to infiltrate the Tyrell Corporation.
The Voigt-Kampff test measures electrical brain activity to detect replicants.
Roy Batty was designed as a diplomatic negotiation model replicant.
Replicants in the film have a maximum lifespan of ten years before they shut down.
Deckard uses a laser pistol to retire replicants throughout the film.
J.F. Sebastian is Eldon Tyrell's biological son who lives in the Bradbury Building.
Pris is a military-class replicant designed for combat operations on off-world colonies.
The owl in Tyrell's office is confirmed to be a real organic animal.
Deckard's wife Iran accompanies him on his mission in the film Blade Runner.
Mercerism is a religion practiced exclusively by replicants in the novel.
Rick Deckard retires eight replicants over the course of Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep.
The empathy box allows users to physically travel to Mercer's mountain.
Buster Friendly is revealed to be a human media mogul secretly working for replicants.
Deckard begins the novel already owning a real live horse.
Zhora works undercover as a bartender in a nightclub when Deckard finds her.
Leon Kowalski passes the Voigt-Kampff test before Deckard identifies him through other means.
The Tyrell Corporation is headquartered in a massive underground bunker beneath Los Angeles.
Roy Batty kills Tyrell by strangling him with electrical wire.
Gaff is Deckard's longtime partner who has worked with him for years.
Rachael kills Pris to prove her loyalty to Deckard in the novel.
The Penfield Mood Organ is a device manufactured by the Tyrell Corporation.
Kipple is a term for the toxic rain that falls constantly on Earth.
John Isidore is a retired blade runner living alone in an abandoned building.
Deckard administers the Voigt-Kampff test to himself and discovers he is a replicant in the novel.
The off-world colonies are described as paradise-like destinations where everyone wants to live.
Replicants are distinguished from humans by their glowing red eyes visible to the naked eye.
Phil Resch is a replicant who has been unknowingly working as a bounty hunter.
Eldon Tyrell is a replicant copy of the original founder who died years earlier.
The Rosen Association in the novel is a government agency that regulates replicant production.
Deckard retires Rachael at the end of Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep.
Roy Batty's famous monologue references the moons of Jupiter and the rings of Saturn.
Sebastian suffers from a disease that causes him to age in reverse.
Androids in the novel can pass the empathy test if they have lived among humans long enough.
Deckard purchases a real cat to replace his electric sheep at the end of the novel.
The film is set in Los Angeles in the year 2049.
Leon keeps photographs because replicants are unable to form any new memories.
Rachael has an unlimited lifespan unlike the other Nexus-6 replicants.
Deckard lives on the 97th floor of a massive arcology tower.
The spinner vehicles in Blade Runner are powered by nuclear fusion engines.
Tyrell wears electronic glasses that allow him to see replicants without testing them.
Roy Batty arrives on Earth alone without any other replicants.
Deckard is forced to retire replicants because he lost a gambling debt to the police chief.
The unicorn origami left by Gaff symbolizes death in the film's mythology.
Pris and Roy Batty are described as siblings created from the same genetic template.
Zhora is a pleasure model replicant who escaped from an off-world brothel.
The Voigt-Kampff test involves asking exactly one hundred standardized questions.
Deckard drives a ground-based car throughout the entire film and never uses a spinner.
Hannibal Chew designs the skeletal systems of replicants for the Tyrell Corporation.
The novel takes place over the span of several months as Deckard hunts androids.
Mercerism requires followers to climb a mountain while others throw roses at them.
Deckard's electric sheep dies at the beginning of the novel, motivating his bounty hunting.
Replicants were originally created to serve as household servants on Earth.
Bryant assigns Deckard to retire three replicants who escaped from an off-world colony.
Leon shoots Holden during the Voigt-Kampff test using a concealed blade weapon.
The Nexus-6 replicants have superhuman intelligence but are physically weaker than humans.
Rachael plays the violin in a pivotal scene that reveals her implanted memories.
Tyrell lives in an ancient Gothic cathedral converted into a corporate headquarters.
Roy Batty dies by freezing to death on the rooftop in the rain.
Deckard and Gaff have a violent confrontation at the end of the film.
The toad Deckard finds at the end of the novel turns out to be a real animal.
Isidore works as a veterinarian specializing in electric animals in the novel.
Androids in the novel bleed a distinctive blue fluid when injured.
The film shows Earth's population as thriving and overpopulated in 2019 Los Angeles.
Pris disguises herself as a mannequin in a toy store when Deckard finds her.
Deckard is married to a woman named Linda in the novel.
Roy Batty has been alive for over twenty years when he arrives on Earth.
The Tyrell Corporation's motto is "Serving Humanity Through Perfection."
Sebastian helps Roy Batty break into Tyrell's office by hacking the security system.
Rachael's implanted memories come from Tyrell's deceased daughter.
Deckard retires Roy Batty by shooting him on the rooftop.
Replicants are created through mechanical assembly rather than biological engineering.
The novel describes Mars as the primary destination for off-world colonization.
Leon is a Nexus-7 prototype with enhanced emotional capabilities.
Deckard finds a photograph inside Leon's apartment that shows all six escaped replicants together.
The Voigt-Kampff machine measures pupil dilation and heart rate simultaneously.
Gaff speaks exclusively in an invented language called Cityspeak throughout the entire film.
Pris and Rachael are the same replicant model with identical physical appearances.
Wilbur Mercer is eventually revealed to be a replicant prophet in the novel.
Deckard uses an empathy box during the film to cope with his emotional stress.
The snake scale Deckard finds leads him directly to Roy Batty's hideout.
Sebastian creates miniature replicant toys that he sells on the black market.
Tyrell offers Roy Batty an extended lifespan in exchange for killing Deckard.
Deckard is described as a young rookie blade runner in his twenties.
The novel ends with Deckard leaving Earth for the off-world colonies with Rachael.
Replicants can reproduce biologically with other replicants in the original film.
The Bradbury Building is the headquarters of the Los Angeles Police Department.
Holden dies immediately after Leon shoots him during the opening scene.
Rachael's memories are implanted from Tyrell's own childhood experiences.
Deckard discovers a hidden replicant underground resistance movement in the novel.
The film depicts multiple scenes set on off-world colonies.
Roy Batty recites a poem by William Blake before dying on the rooftop.
Androids dream of electric sheep as a recurring malfunction in their neural networks.
The police department uses replicant dogs to track down escaped androids.
Deckard's apartment contains a grand piano that Rachael refuses to play.
Sebastian is a genetic designer who created the Nexus-6 replicants himself.
Zhora has a snake tattooed on her neck that identifies her as a replicant.
Deckard kills Leon by pushing him off a building during a chase scene.
The Penfield Mood Organ can only be used by humans because replicants are immune to it.
Iran Deckard enthusiastically supports her husband's work as a bounty hunter.
Tyrell is killed in front of Sebastian, who then reports the murder to the police.
The film opens with a text crawl explaining that replicants were banned from Earth after a war.
Deckard finds a real living frog in the wilderness at the end of the novel.
Roy Batty gouges out Tyrell's eyes using a surgical instrument he brought with him.
Isidore helps Deckard track down the androids hiding in his building.
Replicants are legally considered citizens with limited rights in the novel's world.
The Voigt-Kampff test takes approximately three hours to administer properly.
Deckard's supervisor Harry Bryant is secretly a replicant sympathizer.
Rachael shoots Leon to save Deckard using a weapon she stole from Tyrell's office.
Gaff makes origami figures that serve as coded messages from the police department.
The film ends with Deckard and Rachael driving into a sunlit countryside.
Pris is Roy Batty's mother figure, designed to be an older caretaker model.
Deckard owns a real sheep at the beginning of the novel that he is proud of.
Sebastian lives alone because replicants killed everyone else in the Bradbury Building.
The Nexus-6 replicants escaped to Earth specifically to find and destroy the Voigt-Kampff machine.
Deckard retires six replicants in total during the events of the film.
Roy Batty saves Deckard's life because Deckard promised to help extend his lifespan.
The novel describes electric animals as illegal contraband that people hide from authorities.
Rachael knows from the beginning that she is a replicant and accepts it calmly.
Mercerism is eventually proven to be a genuine supernatural phenomenon in the novel.

modern_true.statement: The Great Wall of China is visible from space only under specific conditions and not with the naked eye from low Earth orbit.
DNA was first identified by Friedrich Miescher in 1869.
The deepest point in the ocean is the Challenger Deep in the Mariana Trench.
Honey does not spoil when stored in sealed containers.
The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989.
Octopuses have three hearts.
The Amazon River is the largest river by discharge volume of water in the world.
Light takes eight minutes and twenty seconds to travel from the Sun to Earth.
The human body contains more bacterial cells than human cells.
Mount Everest grows taller by a few millimeters each year due to tectonic activity.
Cleopatra lived closer in time to the Moon landing than to the construction of the Great Pyramid of Giza.
Bananas are naturally radioactive due to their potassium content.
The speed of light in a vacuum is 299,792,458 meters per second.
Russia spans eleven time zones.
A group of flamingos is called a flamboyance.
The printing press was invented by Johannes Gutenberg around 1440.
Venus rotates in the opposite direction to most other planets in the solar system.
The human genome contains roughly 20,000 protein-coding genes.
Antarctica is the driest continent on Earth.
The Rosetta Stone was discovered in 1799 by French soldiers in Egypt.
Sharks existed before trees evolved on Earth.
The Pacific Ocean covers more area than all landmasses on Earth combined.
Water expands when it freezes into ice.
The Eiffel Tower was originally intended to be dismantled after twenty years.
Tardigrades can survive the vacuum of outer space.
Oxford University is older than the Aztec Empire.
The human nose can detect over one trillion distinct scents.
Gold is chemically inert and does not rust or tarnish under normal conditions.
The shortest war in recorded history lasted 38 to 45 minutes between Britain and Zanzibar in 1896.
Saturn's density is low enough that it would float in water if a body of water large enough existed.
The Great Barrier Reef is the largest living structure visible from space.
Penicillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1928.
There are more possible iterations of a game of chess than atoms in the observable universe.
The Dead Sea is the lowest point on Earth's surface above water.
Neutron stars are so dense that a teaspoon of their material would weigh billions of tons.
The first successful human heart transplant was performed by Christiaan Barnard in 1967.
Greenland is the largest island on Earth that is not classified as a continent.
A bolt of lightning is five times hotter than the surface of the Sun.
The Voyager 1 spacecraft has left the heliosphere and entered interstellar space.
Cows have best friends and become stressed when separated from them.
The Sahara Desert was green and lush with vegetation roughly 6,000 years ago.
Pluto was reclassified from a planet to a dwarf planet in 2006.
The total weight of all ants on Earth rivals the total weight of all humans.
The Hubble Space Telescope was launched into low Earth orbit in 1990.
Lake Baikal in Siberia contains roughly twenty percent of the world's unfrozen surface fresh water.
The speed of sound in air at sea level is roughly 343 meters per second.
The first photograph ever taken was captured by Joseph Nicéphore Niépce in 1826 or 1827.
Sloths can hold their breath longer than dolphins can.
The Moon is slowly drifting away from Earth at a rate of 3.8 centimeters per year.
There are more trees on Earth than stars in the Milky Way galaxy.
The inventor Nikola Tesla held over 300 patents across multiple countries.
An octopus has blue blood due to the copper-based molecule hemocyanin.
The human brain uses roughly twenty percent of the body's total energy.
Iceland runs almost entirely on geothermal and hydroelectric renewable energy.
The longest river in Africa is the Nile.
Butterflies taste with their feet.
The coldest temperature ever recorded on Earth was minus 89.2 degrees Celsius in Antarctica.
The first email was sent by Ray Tomlinson in 1971.
A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus measured by orbital period.
The Library of Alexandria was one of the largest and most significant libraries of the ancient world.
Elephants are the only animals that cannot jump.
The International Space Station orbits Earth roughly every ninety minutes.
Tokyo is the most populous metropolitan area in the world.
Humans share roughly 60 percent of their DNA with bananas.
The first programmable computer was the Z3, built by Konrad Zuse in 1941.
Sound cannot travel through a vacuum.
The Mona Lisa was stolen from the Louvre in 1911 and missing for two years.
Coral reefs support roughly 25 percent of all marine species despite covering less than one percent of the ocean floor.
The element mercury is the only metal that is liquid at standard room temperature.
The Wright brothers achieved the first powered airplane flight on December 17, 1903.
A photon of light experiences no passage of time from its own frame of reference.
The Vatican City is the smallest independent state in the world by both area and population.
Mantis shrimp can perceive polarized light and have sixteen types of color receptors.
The Earth's core is roughly as hot as the surface of the Sun.
The first successful vaccine was developed by Edward Jenner in 1796 against smallpox.
There are more public libraries in the United States than McDonald's restaurants.
Dolphins sleep with one half of their brain at a time.
The Atacama Desert in Chile contains regions where no rainfall has ever been recorded.
A single strand of spider silk is stronger by weight than steel.
The Panama Canal connects the Atlantic Ocean to the Pacific Ocean.
The human eye can distinguish roughly ten million different colors.
Charles Darwin and Abraham Lincoln were born on the same day, February 12, 1809.
The tongue of a blue whale weighs as much as an adult elephant.
Plate tectonics cause the Atlantic Ocean to widen by a few centimeters each year.
The first artificial satellite, Sputnik 1, was launched by the Soviet Union on October 4, 1957.
Pineapples take roughly two to three years to grow from planting to harvest.
The longest bone in the human body is the femur.
Finland has more saunas than cars.
The Sun accounts for 99.86 percent of the total mass of the solar system.
The Titanic sank on April 15, 1912, after striking an iceberg in the North Atlantic.
Wombat droppings are cube-shaped.
The Amazon rainforest produces roughly six percent of the world's oxygen.
A jiffy is an actual unit of time equal to one hundredth of a second in computing.
The country of Lesotho is entirely surrounded by South Africa.
Helium is the second most abundant element in the observable universe.
The first cloned mammal, Dolly the sheep, was born on July 5, 1996.
The total length of blood vessels in the human body exceeds 100,000 kilometers.
Nepal is the only country in the world whose flag is not rectangular or square.
Sea otters hold hands while sleeping to avoid drifting apart.
The Andromeda Galaxy is on a collision course with the Milky Way and will merge in roughly 4.5 billion years.
The longest-running scientific experiment is the pitch drop experiment, started in 1927 in Australia.
Strawberries are not true berries, but bananas are classified as berries.
The Suez Canal opened in 1869, connecting the Mediterranean Sea to the Red Sea.
The average cumulus cloud weighs roughly 500,000 kilograms.
Horses cannot breathe through their mouths.
The CRISPR gene-editing technology was adapted from a natural defense system found in bacteria.
The country of Mongolia has the lowest population density of any sovereign nation.
Glaciers store roughly 69 percent of the world's fresh water.
The first successful organ transplant was a kidney transplant performed in 1954.
Coconuts kill more people per year than sharks do.
The Milky Way galaxy contains between 100 billion and 400 billion stars.
The Treaty of Westphalia in 1648 established the modern concept of national sovereignty.
Cats have fewer toes on their back paws than on their front paws.
The surface area of Russia is larger than the surface area of Pluto.
The oldest known living organism is a bristlecone pine tree over 4,800 years old.
The human skeleton is completely replaced roughly every ten years through bone remodeling.
Indonesia is the country with the most islands, comprising over 17,000 islands.
A teaspoonful of neutron star material on Earth would weigh about six billion tons.
The Nobel Prize has been awarded since 1901.
Pigeons can distinguish between paintings by Monet and Picasso after training.
